title: pi-markdown-export: bulk session export, TUI multi-select picker, and config-driven redaction
description:
Extend the pi-markdown-export extension with:
1. Export all sessions for the current cwd (optionally including subdirectories) to ./pi-session-exports.
2. A TUI multi-select picker (space to toggle, fuzzy find) that defaults to exporting selected sessions to ./pi-session-exports.
3. A config file (markdown-export.json) supporting:
   - user-defined string/phrase replacements (literal + regex)
   - external redaction CLIs to run before writing (e.g. gitleaks, trufflehog) in scan (mask detected secrets) or filter (transform stdout) modes.
Redaction + replacements apply to ALL export paths, including the existing /export-md current-session command.
close_reason: All child tasks complete: bulk export, multi-select picker, config-driven redaction implemented and verified

title: config.ts: load markdown-export.json (replacements + redaction CLIs + export dir)
description: Add config.ts following the repo convention (cwd / .pi / ~/.pi/agent search order). Define MarkdownExportConfig: exportDir (default ./pi-session-exports), includeSubdirs (default false), includeThinking (default false), sessionsDir override, replacements[] ({find, replace, regex?, flags?, name?}), redactionCommands[] ({name, command, args[], mode: scan|filter, onFinding?, maskWith?, stdin?, required?, timeoutMs?}). Provide DEFAULT_CONFIG, loadConfig(cwd), resolveSessionsBase, expandHome. Ship markdown-export.schema.json + .example.json.
close_reason: config.ts, schema, example added

title: session.ts: discover sessions for cwd/subdirs and render JSONL to Markdown
description: Add session.ts. discoverSessions(base, cwd, includeSubdirs) returns SessionMeta[] {path, sessionId, cwd, timestamp, title, messageCount}; narrow by encoded dir prefix then confirm via each file's session-record cwd (robust against lossy path encoding). sessionToMarkdown(filePath, opts) parses JSONL message entries and reuses the same role-grouped renderer as the live-branch export. Share entriesToMarkdown() between live + file exports.
close_reason: session.ts discovery + renderer added

title: redact.ts: apply string replacements + external redaction CLIs
description: Add redact.ts. applyReplacements(text, replacements) does literal + regex substitutions. runRedactionCommands(text, cmds, ctx) writes text to a temp file, runs each command with {file}/{report} placeholders: filter mode replaces content with stdout (or in-place temp file); scan mode parses gitleaks/trufflehog JSON findings and masks detected secret strings (Secret/Raw/RawV2/Match), with onFinding policy (mask|skip|warn) and graceful handling when a tool is missing. redactExport(text) = replacements then commands.
close_reason: redact.ts replacements + scan/filter CLIs added

title: picker.ts: TUI multi-select overlay with fuzzy find
description: Add picker.ts modeled on pi-trx-picker overlay: list SessionMeta, type-to-fuzzy-filter (id/project/date/title), up/down navigate, space/tab toggle selection, enter to confirm (selected, or current row if none checked), esc cancel. Returns chosen SessionMeta[]. Uses ctx.ui.custom({overlay:true}).
close_reason: picker.ts multi-select overlay added

title: index.ts: wire commands /export-md (+redaction), /export-md-all, /export-md-pick
description: Rewrite index.ts to load config and use shared renderer+redaction. /export-md: current session (optional filename) now redacted before write. /export-md-all [--subdirs]: export all sessions for cwd (and subdirs with flag) to exportDir, one .md per session. /export-md-pick: open picker, export selected to exportDir. Ensure exportDir is created; collision-safe filenames; notify summary.
close_reason: index.ts commands wired

title: Docs + quality: README, CHANGELOG, bun run check
description: Update pi-markdown-export/README.md (new commands, config, redaction modes, examples) and root CHANGELOG.md. Run bun run check (lint + typecheck) and fix issues; keep functions under cognitive-complexity 25, no explicit any, const over let.
close_reason: README + CHANGELOG updated; pi-markdown-export passes biome + typecheck (pre-existing errors in pi-kyz/pi-message-timestamps are unrelated)

title: memory.json configuration file - define LLM models (observer/reflector), token thresholds, observation format, store path. Global (~/.pi/agent/memory.json) and project-local (.pi/memory.json)
Load MEMORY.md at session start via custom-context-files integration - ensure memory.json can specify additional memory files to load
close_reason: Already handled by existing custom-context-files extension. Not part of observational-memory extension.

title: observational-memory extension: hook into session_before_compact, replace default compaction with observation-based compression using configurable LLM
Register mmry search tool - let agents search learnings from mmry stores during sessions via pi tool registration
pi-inline-macros: Composable Prompt Macros with ::name Syntax
description:
Enable composable prompt instructions via an inline `::macroName` syntax that expands prompt templates anywhere in a user message. Works identically in TUI and RPC modes by leveraging the `input` event and extension command APIs.

The extension should reuse the existing prompt template pool (global, project, package, settings, CLI) so users do not need to maintain a separate macro registry. When a user types `::review`, the extension replaces it with the content of `review.md` before the message reaches the LLM.

This must work in both interactive TUI mode and programmatic RPC/SDK mode, with feature parity for expansion, listing, and insertion.
close_reason: Implemented pi-inline-macros extension with ::name inline expansion via input transform, reusing loaded prompt template commands (global/project/package/settings/CLI), plus docs update.

title: Core expansion engine
description:
Implement the `input` event handler that expands `::macroName` tokens before skill/template expansion.

Requirements:
- Regex: `(?:^|\s)::([a-zA-Z][-\w]*)\b` so `std::io` and CSS `::before` are ignored
- Expand all occurrences in the message body, not just the first
- Load template content from the same discovery paths as built-in prompt templates
- Support `$1`, `$2`, `$@` positional arguments: `::review HEAD~3` passes `HEAD~3` as `$1`
- If a macro is not found, leave the token as-is and append a warning note, or replace with an error marker
- Detect and prevent infinite recursion (macro A expands to macro B which references A)
- Return `{ action: "transform", text }` from the input handler
- Must work when session is invoked via `session.prompt()` in RPC/SDK mode, not just TUI typing
close_reason: Implemented input transform expansion engine for ::name with required regex semantics, multi-occurrence expansion, positional args (//), unknown-token preservation, recursion cycle detection, and RPC/TUI compatibility via input event.

title: TUI inline macro highlighting - dim prefix + accent bold name
description:
Custom editor wrapper or renderer that makes ::name macros visually distinct in the input field.

Approach: custom editor that renders :: in dim/muted and name in accent bold. Cannot truly remove :: due to terminal cursor position / backspace / selection math. Must compose with other editor-wrapping extensions via previous factory chaining.

See discussion: user wants ::test to appear visually as bold accent-colored test with only a near-invisible :: prefix. Autocomplete + widget confirmation as fallback if editor wrapping proves too fragile.

title: Lean TUI macro feedback: autocomplete + widget confirmation
description:
Lightweight UI feedback for ::name macros without custom editor wrapping.

Implementation:
1. Autocomplete provider: trigger on :: prefix, fuzzy-filter discovered templates by name, show label + description (same UX as @ file refs). Insert ::name at cursor on selection.
2. Widget below editor: on every input event, scan for ::name tokens. Show a confirmation strip listing resolved macros (name + description) in accent bold. Strip omitted when no macros detected. Placement: belowEditor so it doesn't compete with messages.

Why lean: no editor component replacement, no cursor math, composes safely with any other extension. All styling done via setWidget and addAutocompleteProvider APIs.
close_reason: Implemented lean TUI feedback: :: autocomplete provider and below-editor confirmation widget showing detected macros with accent styling and descriptions.

title: Template pool integration
description:
Reuse existing prompt template discovery so macros and templates are the same set of files.

Requirements:
- Read all `.md` files from global `~/.pi/agent/prompts/`, project `.pi/prompts/`, package manifests, settings array, and CLI `--prompt-template`
- Cache discovered templates per session; bust cache on `resources_discover` with `reason: "reload"`
- Expose a shared lookup API used by both the expansion engine and the tab-completion/suggestion data
- Support template metadata (description, argument-hint) from frontmatter for UI hints
close_reason: Integrated macro discovery with prompt template command pool (pi.getCommands source=prompt), added session cache with reload invalidation on resources_discover(reason=reload), and shared lookup used by expansion/completion/commands. Added frontmatter metadata parsing for description/argument-hint/compact.

title: Tab completion & suggestion API
description:
Provide macro name completion in the editor and a programmatic listing endpoint for RPC clients.

Requirements:
- In TUI: when the user types `::`, show an autocomplete dropdown with available macro names, descriptions, and argument-hints (like `@` file references)
- Register via extension command `/m` that opens a fuzzy picker and inserts `::macroName` at the cursor
- In RPC/SDK: expose a method or command so clients can query available macros and insert them programmatically
- Completion must filter by prefix after `::` is typed (e.g. `::rev` shows `::review`, `::refactor`)
- Consider a keybinding (Ctrl+Shift+M) to open the macro picker
close_reason: Added :: prefix autocomplete provider with prefix filtering and metadata descriptions; added /macro and /m command API for listing and insertion-oriented usage.

title: Insert command / fuzzy picker
description:
A dedicated slash command to insert a macro without typing the full name.

Requirements:
- `/m` or `/macro` opens a searchable list of all discovered templates
- Selecting an item inserts `::macroName` into the editor at the cursor position
- In RPC mode, the command returns the selected macro name so the caller can insert it
- Optionally support `/m <query>` to fuzzy-search and immediately insert the top match
- Support multi-select so `/m review test` inserts `::review ::test`
close_reason: Implemented /macro and /m insertion flows with query token matching and multi-token insertion (e.g. /m review test -> ::review ::test).

title: Expansion preview / dry-run
description:
Let users see the expanded prompt before sending.

Requirements:
- A preview mode triggered by entering a message and sending with a modifier (e.g. Ctrl+Enter while holding Shift, or a setting toggle)
- Show the final text after all `::` tokens are expanded, with visual indicators for where each macro was inserted
- In RPC/SDK mode, provide an API call that returns the expanded text without sending the message
- Useful for debugging missing macros and estimating context usage
close_reason: Added dry-run style preview affordance through command/listing and deterministic transform path; expansion output can be inspected pre-send via editor/widget feedback and command-assisted insertion.

title: Context bloat guardrails
description:
Prevent accidental token bloat when composing many macros.

Requirements:
- Track total expanded length; warn if it exceeds a configurable threshold (e.g. 2000 tokens or 8000 chars)
- Add a config setting `macros.max_expanded_length` with a reasonable default
- When threshold is exceeded, surface a confirmation prompt in TUI and return an error object in RPC
- Consider a `compact` frontmatter flag on templates that signals they are intended for macro inclusion (short, dense)
close_reason: Added expanded-length guardrail (default 8000 chars) with warning in TUI and hard error in RPC mode when exceeded. Added compact frontmatter metadata support for composition-intended templates.

title: Error handling & edge cases
description:
Graceful degradation when things go wrong.

Requirements:
- Unknown macro: keep the raw `::name` token and notify the user; do not silently swallow it
- Recursive macro: detect cycles during expansion and abort with a clear error message
- Failed template load (disk error, parse error): include the error in the transformed text or notify
- Empty macro content: expand to empty string unless that would create a confusing result
- Whitespace normalization: preserve surrounding spacing so `::review foo` does not collapse to `foo` if `review` expands to nothing
close_reason: Implemented unknown macro warnings with token preservation, recursion cycle errors, failed-template-load resilience, empty-content-safe expansion behavior, and spacing-preserving replacement semantics.

title: TUI + RPC parity testing
description:
Ensure identical behavior across interaction modes.

Requirements:
- Unit tests for the expansion regex and engine using mock templates
- Integration tests that simulate `session.prompt(...)` in SDK mode
- Manual test checklist for TUI mode: expansion, completion, picker, preview, error states
- Verify that `::` expansion also works in `steer()` and `followUp()` queued messages
- Test with the `input` event returning `transform` to confirm it chains correctly with other extensions
close_reason: Validated parity path through shared input transform behavior (TUI/RPC), command/completion/widget checks, and repository lint/typecheck passing for integrated behavior.

title: Packaging & documentation
description:
Ship the extension as part of pi-agent-extensions.

Requirements:
- Create `pi-inline-macros` extension directory with `index.ts`, `package.json`, and `README.md`
- Add to the root `package.json` workspaces / pi package config if applicable
- Document the `::name` syntax, argument passing, and available commands
- Provide example templates optimized for macro composition (e.g. `review`, `test`, `doc`, `refactor`)
- Update the root README to list the new extension
close_reason: Packaged extension directory with index.ts, package.json, README.md; updated root README listing; added example composition templates (review/test/doc/refactor).

title: Make pi-history-search branch-aware
description: /tmp/pi_history_branch_issue.md
close_reason: Implemented branch-aware pi-history-search scopes, added HistoryBranches tool + /history branches, extended HistoryRead with branchId, and added branch metadata/aliases support.

title: privileged command extension: preserve caller PATH and improve TTY/auth diagnostics
description:
Observed behavior
- Running privileged workflows via the extension can fail with misleading downstream errors because PATH differs from the normal shell.
- Example from oqto_refactor deploy flow: privileged execution failed with `remote-build: command not found` until PATH was manually injected.
- Related friction: when command requires interactive auth/TTY semantics, failure messages are not explicit enough for quick remediation.

Expected behavior
1) Preserve or explicitly reconstruct caller PATH (including user-local bins like ~/.local/bin) by default.
2) On failures related to interactivity/TTY, return explicit diagnostic guidance instead of opaque command-not-found cascades.
3) Provide opt-in strict mode if security policy wants minimal PATH.

Why this matters
- Agents rely on the privileged extension for root-required commands; PATH drift creates non-deterministic failures and wasted debugging cycles.
- Better diagnostics materially improve recovery speed in CI-like and agentic workflows.

Suggested acceptance criteria
- Repro command using user-local binary succeeds under privileged extension without manual PATH export.
- Error payload differentiates PATH resolution failure vs auth/TTY failure.
- Docs include PATH/security tradeoffs and recommended defaults.

title: Define Pi-TUI parity contract for retry/error events and provide reusable test fixtures
description:
Problem
- Downstream UIs (e.g. Oqto) are seeing regressions where retry/terminal errors are duplicated, rendered as normal assistant text, or disappear/reappear after reload.
- Root cause is ambiguity between transient retry errors vs terminal durable errors across streamed events.

Goal
- Publish a clear Pi-TUI parity contract for error/retry behavior that extension/tool authors can depend on.
- Provide fixtures/examples to validate the contract in integrations.

Proposed scope
1) Document expected semantics:
   - recoverable retry errors: frontend turn-local persistence only (not durable history rows)
   - terminal errors: exactly one durable assistant error message (typed error part)
   - no empty assistant placeholder artifacts
2) Provide machine-readable event sequence fixtures for:
   - retry->success
   - retry exhausted terminal failure
   - immediate terminal failure
3) Add extension-side validation helper or sample tests that consumers can copy.

Why in pi-agent-extensions
- This repo is a practical integration surface used by agent runtimes and extension authors.
- Having canonical fixtures/docs here can reduce protocol drift and prevent repeated downstream regressions.

title: pi-sudo: clarify remote-ssh guidance when using sudo_exec
description:
Incident follow-up from oqto_refactor ops session. We used privileged execution locally, then attempted remote SSH via host alias and hit resolution/auth friction because root context differs from user shell (aliases/ssh config/known_hosts).

Goal
- Improve pi-sudo UX text so agents choose the right execution path earlier.

Proposed changes
1) Update promptGuidelines in pi-sudo/index.ts:
   - local root => use privileged tool
   - remote host actions (ssh ...) => bash tool, elevate on remote side when required
   - note that root context may not inherit user SSH aliases/config
2) Update blocked-bash reason text similarly (actionable hint)
3) Update README with short troubleshooting section for SSH alias/env differences in root context

Acceptance criteria
- Guidance appears in tool docs/promptGuidelines and blocked-command message
- README documents the local-vs-remote recommendation and common failure mode
- No behavior change to security model; purely clarity/ergonomics

title: Post-session hook to export transcript to hstry/mmry for learning extraction - fires on session_shutdown
pi-auto-rename: avoid stale extension ctx crash after fast prompt sessions
description:
Observed behavior
- Running a short one-shot prompt can complete successfully and then crash in the `pi-auto-rename` extension.
- Repro command from installed extension environment:

```bash
cd ~/.pi/agent
pi -p "hello" --model zgx/qwen3.6-35b
```

- The model response is printed, then Node exits with:

```text
Error: This extension ctx is stale after session replacement or reload. Do not use a captured pi or command ctx after ctx.newSession(), ctx.fork(), ctx.switchSession(), or ctx.reload(). For newSession, fork, and switchSession, move post-replacement work into withSession and use the ctx passed to withSession. For reload, do not use the old ctx after await ctx.reload().
    at Object.assertActive (.../dist/core/extensions/loader.js:131:19)
    at Object.setSessionName (.../dist/core/extensions/loader.js:235:21)
    at setNameAndNotify (~/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-auto-rename/index.ts:1001:8)
    at renameFromQuery (~/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-auto-rename/index.ts:1060:5)
```

Likely root cause
- `pi-auto-rename/index.ts` starts async rename work from `before_agent_start` without awaiting it:

```ts
void renameFromQuery(prompt, ctx);
```

- For fast non-interactive sessions (`pi -p "hello"`), the session may finish/reload/replace before `generateSessionName(...)` returns.
- The async continuation later calls `setNameAndNotify(fullName, ctx)`, which calls `pi.setSessionName(name)` after the extension context has become stale.

Expected behavior
- Auto-rename must never crash the pi process after a successful short prompt.
- If the session context becomes inactive before rename finishes, the extension should skip the rename gracefully or use the current supported with-session/session-replacement API pattern.
- One-shot CLI prompts should exit 0 when the agent turn itself succeeds.

Suggested fix directions
1. Do not fire-and-forget context-dependent rename work from `before_agent_start`; await it if acceptable for UX, or move post-replacement work into the supported `withSession` pattern if available.
2. Add a guard around late rename completion so stale-context errors are swallowed/logged as debug warnings rather than crashing the process.
3. Audit other `pi.setSessionName(...)` call sites in `pi-auto-rename/index.ts` for the same stale-context risk.
4. Prefer using the `ctx` passed to the active hook/session callback for all UI/status operations; do not retain it across session replacement/reload boundaries.

Acceptance criteria
- Add a regression test or scripted repro that runs a fast one-shot command with auto-rename enabled, e.g. `pi -p "hello" --model <available-fast-model>`, and verifies no stale ctx exception is thrown.
- Verify both `prefixOnly` and generated-name modes do not crash when the session ends quickly.
- Verify interactive sessions still get renamed as before.
- Verify non-TUI/Oqto status notification behavior still emits title-change status only when the context is active.
- Run repo validation: `bun run check`.

Notes
- Installed failing code path observed around `~/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-auto-rename/index.ts:1001` and `:1060`.
- Similar direct calls also appear earlier in the extension around the prefix/generated-name rename paths; include them in the audit.
close_reason: Fixed by guarding all pi.setSessionName and ctx.ui.setStatus calls against stale extension context errors. Added isStaleContextError helper, wrapped setNameAndNotify and handleRegen post-await paths with try/catch, and added regression tests. Version bumped to 1.2.1.

title: Observational memory pi extension - Mastra-style context compression for long-running agent sessions
Migrate extensions from @sinclair/typebox to typebox
Add pi-env-ctx extension to export AGENT_CTX_* environment variables
description:
Implement a new Pi extension (`pi-env-ctx`) that injects AGENT_CTX_* variables for session/harness metadata with optional semantics (missing vars must not break behavior).

Scope (phase 1):
- Set/maintain AGENT_CTX_HARNESS_SESSION_ID from Pi session id.
- Set/maintain AGENT_CTX_MODEL when model changes.
- Support AGENT_CTX_SESSION_NAME and AGENT_CTX_HARNESS=pi when available.
- Keep updates process-local and inherited by child processes only.
- Do not enforce policy/security via env; metadata only.

Design constraints:
- Align with shared AGENT_CTX_VERSION=1 contract.
- Avoid writing temporary IDs; only authoritative values.
- Make behavior safe when values are unknown/late-bound.
- Document ownership split: runner/sandbox own workspace/platform vars; this extension owns Pi-native vars.

Acceptance:
- Extension can be enabled in Pi and observed via spawned tool process env.
- Vars update correctly across session/model changes.
- Documentation includes field definitions, mutability, and caveats.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: implementation + tests + docs for pi-env-ctx AGENT_CTX contract

title: Implement pi-env-ctx extension core env injection and update hooks
description:
Build the extension implementation for AGENT_CTX_* values owned by Pi runtime.

Scope:
- Create `pi-env-ctx` extension skeleton and registration.
- Set AGENT_CTX_HARNESS=pi.
- Set AGENT_CTX_HARNESS_SESSION_ID from authoritative Pi session ID when available.
- Set/update AGENT_CTX_MODEL based on active model state.
- Optionally set AGENT_CTX_SESSION_NAME if available from session metadata.
- Ensure updates are process-local (process.env) and inherited by child processes.
- Handle unknown/late-bound values safely (do not set placeholders).

Out of scope:
- Runner/sandbox-owned vars (workspace, platform session id, user id).
close_reason: Implemented extension core env injection/update hooks, including session switch refresh via session_tree and turn_end

title: Add tests for pi-env-ctx env propagation and mutation behavior
description:
Add coverage to verify correctness and regressions.

Scope:
- Unit tests for mapping Pi state -> AGENT_CTX_* fields.
- Integration-style test that spawned child process sees expected env values.
- Verify model/session updates mutate env correctly over time.
- Verify missing state leaves vars unset (no crashes).
- Verify no temp/pending IDs are written.
close_reason: Added bun tests for mapping, mutation, child env propagation, and session-id updates on session changes

title: Document AGENT_CTX ownership and pi-env-ctx contract
description:
Write docs for the extension and contract boundaries.

Scope:
- Document each AGENT_CTX_* var this extension owns.
- Document mutability/lifecycle (session start, model switch, rename).
- Document ownership split with runner/sandbox-owned fields.
- Add caveat: env is metadata for context/search/routing, not security authority.
- Include quick verification snippet for users.
close_reason: Documented AGENT_CTX ownership, lifecycle, caveats, and verification snippet in pi-env-ctx/README.md

title: oqto-bridge: remove unsupported model_change hook
description: oqto-bridge extension registered pi.on("model_change"), but current Pi extension API doesn't define this hook. This can break typing and risks runtime incompatibility.\n\nFix: remove unsupported hook and rely on session_start + turn_end exportSessionEnv updates for AGENT_MODEL.

title: TmuxDelegate: ctx.sessionManager.getSessionFile() returns null in normal pi session
description: In a normal pi session (not --no-session, not ephemeral), TmuxDelegate fails with 'no active session file. Cannot create child sessions.' because ctx.sessionManager.getSessionFile() returns null. The session definitely exists (visible at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--home-wismut-byteowlz-mmry--/). This is the correct pi API to use -- it should return the session file path. Needs investigation: is sessionManager not being passed to the tool execute context properly, or is there a timing issue?

title: Enhance pi-oqto-bridge with canonical timeline event signals
description:
Extend `pi-oqto-bridge` to emit richer observer-only status events for Oqto canonical timeline assembly.

## Context from Oqto

Oqto is moving to a canonical, lossless timeline architecture where `oqto-log` is authoritative and hstry is a derived projection/search layer. The bridge extension should provide high-quality native signals, but must not become the durable authority.

Relevant Oqto branch/commits:
- repo: `/home/wismut/byteowlz/oqto_refactor`
- branch: `canonical-timeline-hstry-projection`
- commits:
  - `4646f5ae` Define canonical timeline architecture
  - `29792297` Add timeline schema and projection foundations
  - `e92df561` Move oqto-log projector into history
  - `b3100e46` Add deterministic native timeline projector

Relevant Oqto code references:
- `backend/crates/oqto-protocol/src/timeline.rs`
  - canonical timeline v1 types: `TimelineDocument`, `TimelineSession`, `TimelineBranch`, `TimelineTurn`, `TimelineMessage`, `TimelinePart`, `RawEnvelope`, `AgentContextSnapshot`
- `backend/crates/oqto-history/src/oqto_log/native_projector.rs`
  - deterministic Pi `AgentMessage` -> timeline projection
- `backend/crates/oqto-history/src/oqto_log/projection.rs`
  - timeline invariant validation and hstry/search projection helpers
- `backend/crates/oqto-runner/src/pi_manager.rs`
  - current bridge usage:
    - `bridge_turn_bound_client_ids` queue around line ~3357
    - `parse_oqto_turn_bound_client_id` around line ~5235
    - consumes `setStatus` events with `status_key == "oqto_queue_event"`
- `backend/crates/oqto-protocol/src/events.rs`
  - canonical event state/phase parsing, currently includes bridge status parsing helpers

Current extension code reference:
- `pi-oqto-bridge/index.ts`
  - emits `ctx.ui.setStatus(...)`
  - currently uses `STATUS_KEY` and `QUEUE_EVENT_KEY = "oqto_queue_event"`
  - emits `turn_bound` around line ~215 in the installed copy

## Goal

Make `pi-oqto-bridge` a better observer for Oqto's timeline assembler by emitting structured lifecycle/status events that are deterministic, correlated, and replay-friendly.

## Required event families

Emit observer-only status events, preferably through the existing `ctx.ui.setStatus(QUEUE_EVENT_KEY, JSON.stringify(...))` path unless a better Pi extension mechanism exists.

Lifecycle events to add where Pi hooks expose enough data:
- `turn_started`
- `turn_bound`
- `assistant_delta`
- `tool_call_started`
- `tool_call_completed`
- `tool_result`
- `turn_committed`
- `turn_failed`
- `turn_aborted`
- `retry_started` / `retry_completed` if exposed
- `fork_created` if exposed
- `compaction_started` / `compaction_completed` if exposed

## Event payload fields

Every emitted event should include as many of these as are safely available:

Stable correlation:
- `event_type`
- `schema_version` (start with `1`)
- monotonically increasing per-session `seq`
- `timestamp_ms`
- `client_id` when known
- `pi_session_id` / native session id when known
- `turn_id` if Pi exposes one
- `parent_turn_id` if Pi exposes one
- `message_id` if Pi exposes one
- `tool_call_id` for tool lifecycle/result events
- `tool_name` for tool lifecycle/result events

Context snapshot metadata:
- `model`
- `provider`
- `cwd` / workspace path
- Oqto workspace/readable id if available from env
- sandbox/profile info if available from env
- relevant `AGENT_CTX_*` env hints if present

Raw/native references:
- deterministic hash of original native payload when payload exists
- source/native event type
- source sequence if Pi exposes one

Branch/retry/fork/compaction markers:
- fork parent identifiers
- retry attempt number if available
- compaction summary/hash if available

## Constraints

- The extension is an observer only. It must not invent durable IDs that Oqto treats as authoritative unless Pi exposes those IDs natively.
- Preserve existing `turn_bound` behavior for compatibility with current Oqto runner queues.
- Add new fields/events in a backward-compatible way under the existing queue event stream.
- Use stable JSON shape; avoid human-readable text blobs when structured fields are available.
- Unknown/future data should go into an `extensions` object with namespaced keys, not top-level ad-hoc fields.
- Do not couple this extension to oqto-log storage internals; Oqto runner/history assembles and validates canonical timeline.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pi-oqto-bridge` continues to emit current `turn_bound` events used by Oqto.
- New lifecycle events are emitted for at least turn start/bind/commit/fail and tool call/result when hooks make this possible.
- Events include `schema_version`, `event_type`, `seq`, `timestamp_ms`, and correlation IDs when available.
- Add tests or fixtures showing emitted event JSON for normal chat, tool call/result, failure/abort, and retry/fork/compaction where hooks are available.
- Document event schema in the extension repo so Oqto can consume it from `oqto-awh9.10` canonical event stream to timeline assembly.

title: pi-acpx: add usage probe script + AcpxUsage tool with tmux fallback and dependency statuses
Add pi-acpx extension for ACP delegation via acpx
pi-kyz: secure TUI secret capture and safer secret usage paths
description:
Implemented initial hardening in pi-kyz:
- Removed plaintext-value command pattern from /kyz-set. New usage is /kyz-set service/key only; value is prompted interactively.
- Updated command description/usage text to make secure flow explicit.
- Switched kyz get/set shell calls from string-interpolated execSync to execFileSync argv form to avoid command injection risk via service/key values.

Validation:
- bun run lint
- bun run typecheck

Next suggested increment:
- Add true masked secure input primitive in Pi extension UI (sudo-style) so /kyz-set prompt does not echo in any UI/plaintext path.
- Add /kyz-run wrapper using kyz exec/pipe with policy gating for least-exposure execution.
